steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the text suggests about the value of Morales and Vogel's research. The passage says their "unique contribution" is reconstructing the earliest extant annual fiscal records, which show Philip's defaults were caused by short-term liquidity crises, not long-term unsustainable debts. The answer must reflect that their new evidence rules out one explanation and supports another.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Morales and Vogel's research revealed the role of Philip II's debt defaults in exacerbating short-term liquidity crises.’: This reverses the causal direction. The passage says liquidity crises caused the defaults, not the other way around. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Morales and Vogel's research uses newly compiled evidence to eliminate one possible reason Philip II defaulted on his debts and to affirm another.’: The reconstructed records (newly compiled evidence) rule out long-term unsustainable debts and affirm short-term liquidity crises. This matches the passage exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Morales and Vogel's research builds on earlier work about a particular aspect of European early modern state finance by Hansen and Ortega and corrects errors in that earlier work.’: The passage does not say Morales and Vogel correct errors in Hansen and Ortega's work. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Morales and Vogel's research presented a novel body of evidence supporting Hansen and Ortega's hypothesis regarding why Philip II defaulted on several loans.’: The passage does not say Hansen and Ortega had a hypothesis about Philip's defaults, and Morales and Vogel's conclusion is original, not a confirmation of prior work. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
